pr.title: Deepen primer coverage and add saveable interactive lesson practice
pr.body:
The curriculum was too shallow relative to the System Design Primer and lacked any real practice flow beyond passive reading. This change expands the content with deeper distributed-systems coverage and adds per-lesson interactive drafting so learners can save answers locally before moving through prompts.

- **Curriculum depth**
  - Adds a new **Distributed systems building blocks** module covering:
    - consistent hashing and hot-key management
    - consensus, quorums, and leader election
    - distributed transactions, sagas, and idempotent workflows
    - probabilistic data structures and cardinality estimation
    - batch processing, stream processing, and MapReduce
  - Updates site-level copy to reflect broader primer coverage and a practice-oriented workflow.

- **Interactive practice flow**
  - Introduces a reusable `PracticeWorkspace` on lesson pages.
  - Generates a 3-step practice deck per lesson (`opening`, `design`, `tradeoffs`) from lesson metadata.
  - Requires learners to save an answer before advancing to the next prompt.
  - Keeps answers ungraded by design.

- **Local persistence**
  - Adds a dedicated `practiceAnswers` store backed by `localStorage`.
  - Saves answers per lesson/prompt so learners can leave and resume later.
  - Supports clearing saved answers for a lesson without affecting completion progress.

- **Content-model support**
  - Extends `courseData.js` with `getLessonPracticeSteps()` to derive structured practice prompts from existing lesson sections, checklists, pitfalls, and interview prompts.
  - Keeps the practice system data-driven so new lessons inherit the same interaction model automatically.

```js
export function getLessonPracticeSteps(lesson) {
  return [
    { id: 'opening', title: 'Explain the concept', ... },
    { id: 'design', title: 'System design drill', ... },
    { id: 'tradeoffs', title: 'Trade-offs and failure review', ... }
  ];
}
```
pr.author: Copilot
